message:
fix(tests): relax streaming hostcall assertions to tolerate JS-queued tool requests

The streaming exec hostcall tests asserted exact pending_hostcall_count
values after completing stream-based exec calls. However, when JS stream
callbacks (e.g. __b_chunk, __b_done, __right_chunk, __right_done) run,
they can queue additional tool-request hostcalls that inflate the pending
count beyond what the test expected.

Changes:
- extension_dispatcher: relax minimum chunk count from 3→2 for async
  iterator test and aggregate stdout content instead of asserting
  per-chunk ordering (chunks may coalesce across OS scheduling)
- extension_dispatcher: fix binary-stream-replacement-char test to use
  async IIFE pattern with explicit outputDone flag, preventing the
  assertion from racing the stream collection
- streaming_hostcall: replace rigid assert_eq!(pending_hostcall_count, N)
  with targeted assert!(is_hostcall_pending(&call_id)) checks that
  verify the correct exec hostcall lifecycle without coupling to
  transient JS callback tool-request counts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
